name: M. L. Richter, Jr.
motto: "A docile disposition will with difficulty surrender every difficulty."
major: B. S. (E. E.) Univ. of Georgia, 1904
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Tuesday Evening Dining Club

name: John Christian Koch
motto: "There's no genius free from some tincture of madness."
major: B. S. (C. E.) Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Don Roland Bower
motto: "What a pestilent knave is this same."

name: N. H. Bullard
motto: "A thing of beauty is a joy forever."

name: Max Michael
motto: "Much study is a weariness of the flesh."

name: Ernest Lee Worsham
motto: "The devil knew not what he did when he made man politic."

name: Robert Preston Brooks
motto: "A lover is a man who, in his anxiety to possess another, has lost possession of himself."
clubs: Winner H. B. Russell Psychology Prize, 1903

name: Roy Jackson
motto: "Can there be so fair a creature formed of common clay."

name: Isaac Wayne Chandler
motto: "He has a face like a benediction."

name: J. Cohen Chandler, U. P. L.
motto: "Thy modesty's candle to thy merit."
enrolltime: Entered Freshman; B. S. (Ag.) Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: J. E. Craigmiles
motto: "Empty heads console with empty sound."

name: C. R. Oliver, U. P. L.
motto: "For virtue's self may too much zeal be had, The worst of madness is a saint run mad."
enrolltime: Entered Freshman; A. B. Univ. of Georgia, 1904
clubs: Vice-President and President College Y. M. C. A.

name: H. F. Scott
motto: "Whate'er he did, was done with so much ease In him alone 'twas natural to please."
clubs: Editor-in-Chief and Local Editor "Red and Black"

name: Winship Nunnally
motto: "A lion among ladies is a dreadful thing, for there is not a more fearful wild fowl than your lion living."
major: X. Φ., A. B. Class of 1904
clubs: Captain and Manager Senior Track Team

name: H. Quigg Fletcher
motto: "Not all her arts my steady soul shall move, And she shall find indifference conquers love."
major: K. Σ., A. B. Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: W. W. Cook
motto: "Fresh as a bridegroom and his chin new reaped, show'd like a stubble-land at harvest home."

name: T. R. Aycock
motto: "In truth he is a bashful youth."

name: W. E. Ragan, Jr.
motto: "A smile, a ghastly withering smile."

name: Jack D. Bower
motto: "Not to know me is to argue thyself unknown."

name: Herbert L. Reynolds
motto: "The helpless look of blooming infancy."
clubs: President and Treasurer of Phi Kappa Society

name: Geo. P. Whitman
motto: "The glass of fashion and the world of form."
clubs: President College Y. M. C. A.

name: William Otis Roberts
motto: "The world must have great minds, even as great spheres, suns."

name: Paul Lovejoy
motto: "Love's a capricious power."
clubs: Treasurer Sophomore, Junior and Senior Baseball Teams

name: Frank Butner Anderson
motto: "He was a man, take him all in all, I shall not look upon his like again."

name: Gordon C. Carson
motto: "Lo! all the elements of love are here, The smile, the blush, the sigh, the tear."
clubs: 4th Sergeant and 1st Lieutenant, Corps of Cadets

name: Sam J. Crowe
motto: "Doubt that the stars are fire, Doubt truth to be a liar, But never doubt I love."
enrolltime: Entered Junior Class from Emory College

name: Abraham Falk, Jr.
motto: "He is a man of unbounded stomach."

name: Reid, William L.
motto: "Confusion now hath wrought his masterpiece."
major: B. S. (C. E.) Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Harris, Herman H.
motto: "He has I know not what of greatness in his look, and of high pate—, That almost awes me."

name: Rambo, Marcellus
motto: "He trudged along unknowing what he sought, And whistled as he went for want of thought."

name: Ralph Meldrim
motto: "All that dreams can picture, or youthful maidens fancy, when they love."

name: Otto T. Harper
motto: "Innocence is always unsuspicious."

name: B. S. Dobbs
motto: "Go to, you are a child."

name: Alvin G. Golucke
motto: "Lend thy serious hearing to what I shall unfold."

name: George E. Simpson
motto: “Restless ambition, never at a stand.”
major: B. S., Mercer; B. L. Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Isaac S. Peebles
motto: “The ghost of many a veteran bill shall hover around his slumbers.”

name: Thomas Whipple Connally
motto: “He hath never fed on the dainties that are in a book.”
major: Ph. B., Emory College, 1902; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: I. S. Hopkins, Jr.
motto: "Let me have men about me who are fat—Sleek headed men, and such as sleep o'nights."
major: A. B., Emory College, 1901; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904
clubs: Member of Georgia Law Debating Society
Athletic Editor "Red and Black," First Term

name: John B. Gamble
motto: "He draweth out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument."

name: Theo. T. Turnbull
motto: "Yon Cassius hath a lean and hungry look."
major: A. B., Emory College, 1902; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Reynold M. Burt
motto: "Rare compound of oddity, frolic and fun, To relish a joke and laugh at a pun."
clubs: President, Vice-President and Parliamentarian of Demosthenian Society
Secretary and Treasurer and Historian Georgia Law Debating Society

name: Graham D. Perdue
motto: "It is but poor eloquence that only shows that the orator can talk."
major: A. B., Univ. of Georgia; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Georgia Law Debating Society

name: Marmaduke H. Blackshear
motto: "See what a grace is seated on that brow."
major: A. B., Emory College; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Walter Barnett Shaw
motto: "He'd undertake to prove by force of argument, a man's no horse."

name: Richard P. Spencer, Jr.
motto: "Then to the spicy nut brown ale."
major: B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904; Studied at University of Virginia; (Legal)
clubs: Member of Georgia Law Debating Society

name: Robley Dunglison Smith, Jr.
motto: "Haste is of the devil."

name: Robert A. Ashford
motto: "Costly thy habit as thy purse can buy."

name: Edgar R. Gunn
motto: "In the spring a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love."
major: B. L., Emory College; B. L., Univ. of Georgia, 1904

name: Charles C. Bunn, Jr.
motto: "Studious he sat with all his books around, Sinking from thought to thought, a vast profound."

name: Lucien Prichard Goodrich
motto: "Fie! What a spendthrift he is of his tongue."
major: (Legal), A. B., Univ. of Georgia, 1902; B. L., 1904
